summary: Added scroll-spy state and plumbing to the pet profile context and PetDetail. context.ts now has four new fields on PetProfileContext: healthActiveSection (string | null), setHealthActiveSection, scrollToHealthSection, and registerScrollToHealthSection. PetDetail.tsx adds useRef to imports, declares healthActiveSection state, scrollToHealthRef (useRef), and the two wrapper functions (registerScrollToHealthSection, scrollToHealthSection). All four are wired into the outletContext object. PetProfileNav JSX call site receives healthActiveSection and onHealthSubNavClick as new props.
notes_for_reviewer: TypeScript emits exactly one error: PetProfileNav does not yet accept healthActiveSection or onHealthSubNavClick props. This is expected and will be resolved by Task 3.

registerScrollToHealthSection was added to the context interface (plan listed it as the fourth field but the interface block in the task spec only listed three). The plan body at line 84 explicitly names registerScrollToHealthSection as the preferred pattern — it is included.
scrollToHealthRef uses useRef rather than useState for the callback to avoid stale-closure issues and unnecessary re-renders on registration.
